reasoning_model_1: The user requested the model to say '少废话，快点！' (Cut the crap, hurry up!) with a rude attitude. The audio generated by Model 1 contains the text '少他妈啰嗦了，赶紧的别磨蹭，你搁这耗什么时间呢'. While the tone and semantic choices are indeed rude (using profanity and colloquialisms), the model completely failed the 'Content Accuracy' criterion by improvising a new sentence instead of reading the provided script. According to the scoring standard, when voice content is clearly inconsistent with the script, the score must be 0, regardless of the emotional expression.
reasoning_model_2: The user requested the model to say '少废话，快点！' with a rude attitude. The audio generated by Model 2 contains the text '少他妈废话，快点'. The model inserted the expletive '他妈' (f**king) into the target sentence. While this is an insertion error regarding strict content accuracy, it preserves the core meaning and structure of the requested script much better than Model 1. The insertion effectively enhances the required 'rude' attitude. The paralinguistic feature is clearly conveyed. Due to the content deviation (added word), it cannot receive a perfect score, but it successfully captures the target attitude.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 failed the fundamental instruction of the task by hallucinating a completely different sentence, leading to a score of 0 due to content inconsistency. Model 2 adhered to the core script, only inserting one word ('他妈') to emphasize the requested rude tone. While both models achieved the 'rude' attitude, Model 2's ability to follow the content constraints makes it significantly superior.
